content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data," but RunSessionManager.swift:1-14 imports CoreLocation, requests when-in-use authorization, and appends every CLLocation update to `route`, while description.txt advertises "maps every run with GPS."
Pierre: Apple's reviewers cross-check policy claims against actual SDK usage and marketing copy, and a direct contradiction like this is an easy rejection trigger under 5.1.1(i). Here the code clearly collects and stores GPS route data on-device (and possibly iCloud), yet the policy flatly denies any location collection — this must be fixed before submission by updating the privacy policy to accurately describe location/GPS collection and its purpose (and reconciling it with the App Privacy nutrition label).
